mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding economic activity, which lifts corporate earnings and supports equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between month-over-month changes in INDPRO and subsequent 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls to very low levels, it signals labor market tightness that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, depressing equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical pattern of market peaks preceding tightening cycles
failure_condition: More than 40% of triggered instances produce a positive 20-day forward SPX return, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Low interest rates combined with rising housing starts indicate accommodative financial conditions that boost housing investment and consumer wealth, lifting equities over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward SPX return in triggered periods is not statistically different from zero (one-sample t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In low-growth regimes (GDPC1 year-over-year growth below 2%), accelerating retail sales signal a pick-up in demand that supports equity prices; the same signal reverses in high-growth regimes.
failure_condition: During triggered low-growth periods, the rank information coefficient between RSAFS growth and forward 20-day returns does not exceed 0.10 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp one-month acceleration in retail sales above a high threshold indicates vigorous consumer spending that drives short-term equity gains within the next week.
failure_condition: The hit rate (fraction of positive 5-day forward returns) in triggered periods falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) is negative and declining, it provides exceptionally accommodative monetary conditions that re-rate equity valuations higher.
failure_condition: The mean 20-day forward SPX return in triggered periods is not positive at the one-tailed 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained economic momentum that supports corporate earnings and equity prices over a quarter.
failure_condition: The Pearson correlation between monthly payroll changes and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is less than 0.1 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong GDP growth (above 3% year-over-year), continued payroll expansion signals labor market overheating that forces the Fed to tighten, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: During triggered high-growth periods, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10).
